title: supergateway-rs: Rust Rewrite using Asupersync
description:
Rewrite supergateway (~2,100 LOC TypeScript MCP transport bridge) in Rust using Asupersync.
Drop-in CLI replacement bridging MCP stdio servers with SSE, Streamable HTTP, and WebSocket transports.

6 transport modes: stdio→SSE, stdio→WS, stdio→Stateful HTTP, stdio→Stateless HTTP, SSE→stdio, HTTP→stdio.

Targets: <3MB RSS idle, <2ms p99 bridge latency, >10k msg/sec throughput, zero leaked children/FDs.

Source: /c/work/supergateway-rs/PRD.md (v6)

title: US-000: Asupersync Web Stack Validation Spike
description:
GO/NO-GO GATE — Validate Asupersync can serve the required workloads before committing to the rewrite.

This is a time-boxed spike (1-2 days) to build small proof-of-concept programs exercising every Asupersync capability the project needs. Each item is marked works / works-with-fallback / blocked. If any critical item is blocked with no fallback, the project does not proceed.

## Tech Stack
- Runtime: Asupersync 0.2.7 (NOT Tokio)
- Nightly Rust (pin in rust-toolchain.toml)
- All async operations use `&mut Cx` context

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HTTP server: POST/GET/DELETE routes via `asupersync::web::Router` or `App::builder()`
- [ ] SSE streaming: push events one-at-a-time over long-lived connection. If batch-only, demonstrate raw chunked `text/event-stream`
- [ ] WebSocket: upgrade via `WebSocketUpgrade` extractor or manual HTTP upgrade + `net::websocket`. Bidirectional exchange
- [ ] HTTP client: GET and POST via `asupersync::http` or raw TCP
- [ ] SSE client: connect, receive streamed events
- [ ] Child process: determine if `asupersync::process::Command` exists. If not, demonstrate `std::process::Command` with async I/O adapter inside a Region
- [ ] Verify `kill_on_drop` or equivalent — child killed when Region closes
- [ ] Verify `&mut Cx` vs `&Cx` in handler signatures. **Critical:** Determine how concurrent HTTP request handlers each get their own `&mut Cx`. Does the framework fork/split contexts internally? This is a blocking architectural question
- [ ] Timer: `asupersync::time::timeout` wrapping a future
- [ ] Region lifecycle: spawn tasks, cancel region, verify cleanup
- [ ] Sync primitives: `mutex.lock(&cx).await?` works as documented
- [ ] Evaluate `fastmcp_rust` — reusable MCP handling? Document findings
- [ ] LabRuntime: deterministic test with virtual time works
- [ ] Nightly Rust: pin toolchain version, verify Docker build
- [ ] **Go/no-go documented.** For each item: works / works-with-fallback / blocked. If any critical item is blocked with no fallback, project does not proceed

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
notes:
Deliverable: Create spike/FINDINGS.md with a table:
| Item | Status | Notes | Fallback Approach |
Status values: works / works-with-fallback / blocked

Each US-005+ bead references "per US-000 findings" for API availability decisions.
The findings file MUST be at a predictable path for downstream beads to consume.

title: US-009: Signal Handling and Graceful Shutdown
description:
Signal handling for graceful shutdown across all modes. Manages SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP, child cleanup, and PID 1 zombie reaping.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync signal handling (per US-000 findings), else `nix::sys::signal`
- Integration with Region cancellation for cascading cleanup

## File: src/signal.rs

## Signal Handling
- SIGTERM: initiate graceful shutdown
- SIGINT: same as SIGTERM (Ctrl+C)
- SIGHUP: same as SIGTERM (treat as shutdown, not reload)
- On signal received:
  1. Log signal at info level
  2. Cancel top-level Region (cascades to all child Regions → triggers ChildBridge kill sequence: SIGTERM→5s→SIGKILL→drain 1MB→close FDs)
  3. Stop accepting new connections
  4. Wait for in-flight requests to drain (up to 5s per US-006 session drain)
  5. Overall shutdown timeout: 10s backstop. If not cleanly shut down after 10s, force exit(1)

Note: The 10s backstop is a safety net not explicitly in the PRD but prevents indefinite hangs.

## Exit Codes
- 0: clean shutdown (signal received, all children exited)
- 1: error (child spawn failure, bind failure, unhandled error)
- 1: forced shutdown (10s backstop exceeded)

## Stdin EOF Detection (ALL Modes)
In ALL modes, detect stdin EOF as a shutdown trigger:
- Server modes: spawn a dedicated stdin reader task. On EOF, trigger same shutdown as SIGTERM
- Client modes: stdin EOF detected by the existing stdin reader task that reads MCP messages. On EOF, trigger graceful shutdown
- This handles the case where the parent process closes stdin (e.g., IDE closes)
- PRD says stdin EOF is "critical for client modes where parent process may close stdin"

## Cooperative Cancellation
Use `cx.checkpoint()?` at all cancellation points in the shutdown path. This ensures Asupersync cooperative cancellation works correctly when Region.cancel() is called.

## PID 1 Zombie Reaping
When running as PID 1 (Docker container):
- Install SIGCHLD handler with `waitpid(-1, WNOHANG)` loop
- Reap ALL zombie children, not just our direct child
- This is the ONLY place zombie reaping happens — ChildBridge (US-005) only reaps its own child
- Check `getpid() == 1` at startup to enable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SIGTERM handler: cancel top-level Region, drain, exit
- [ ] SIGINT handler: same as SIGTERM
- [ ] SIGHUP handler: same as SIGTERM (not reload)
- [ ] 10s overall shutdown backstop: force exit(1) if not clean
- [ ] Stdin EOF detection in ALL modes (server and client): trigger shutdown on EOF
- [ ] cx.checkpoint()? at cancellation points
- [ ] Exit code 0 for clean shutdown, 1 for error/forced
- [ ] PID 1 zombie reaping: SIGCHLD handler with waitpid(-1, WNOHANG) loop
- [ ] Child processes killed via Region cancellation cascade
- [ ] Integration test: send SIGTERM, verify child killed within 2s
- [ ] Integration test: stdin close → same behavior as SIGTERM
- [ ] Integration test: verify /proc/<pid>/fd count returns to baseline after shutdown
- [ ] Integration test: PID 1 zombie reaping (if testable)

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings

title: US-010: Gateway — stdio->Streamable HTTP (Stateful)
description:
Gateway mode: stdio→Streamable HTTP (stateful). Per-session child process, full MCP session lifecycle with Mcp-Session-Id header.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync HTTP server (per US-000 findings)
- Session manager (US-006) for session lifecycle
- ChildBridge (US-005) spawned per session
- GatewayError (US-003a) for error responses
- Codec (US-004) for child stdin/stdout
- Logger + Metrics (US-008) for observability
- Signal handler (US-009) for shutdown

## File: src/gateway/stdio_to_stateful_http.rs

## Endpoints
- `POST /mcp` (configurable via --streamableHttpPath): JSON-RPC request/notification
- `GET /mcp`: SSE stream for server-initiated messages
- `DELETE /mcp`: terminate session
- Health endpoints as configured by --healthEndpoint

## Protocol Version Passthrough
Client's `protocolVersion` in initialize params is forwarded EXACTLY to child — do not validate or reject. The PRD says "protocol version passthrough" not "validation". If protocol version checking is needed, it applies to the `Latest-Protocol-Version` HTTP header, not the JSON-RPC params.

## POST Handler
1. Validate Content-Type: application/json (else 415)
2. Validate Accept: application/json, text/event-stream (else 406)
3. Parse JSON body (malformed → 400 plain text via GatewayError::bad_request())
4. Body size limit: 16MB. Reject with 413 if exceeded.
5. If no Mcp-Session-Id header AND message is initialize request: create new session
6. If Mcp-Session-Id header: look up session (not found → 404, closing → 503)
7. Acquire SessionAccessGuard (RAII counter — held until response terminal state)
8. Write message(s) to child stdin via codec
9. If request (has id): respond with SSE stream, send response events as `event: message\ndata: <json>\n\n`
10. If notification only (no id): return 202 Accepted immediately
11. If batch: write all to child, return SSE stream with responses for all requests

## Notification Buffering During Init
If child emits notifications before the initialize response arrives, buffer them and forward to the GET SSE stream after the initialize response is sent.

## POST Response Correlation
Multiple POSTs may be in-flight for same session concurrently.
Each POST with request id(s) gets its own SSE stream response.
Correlate child stdout responses to correct POST's SSE stream by matching JSON-RPC id.
Notifications from child go to GET SSE stream (not POST response streams).

## GET Handler (SSE Stream)
1. Validate Accept: text/event-stream
2. Require Mcp-Session-Id header (else 400)
3. Look up session (not found → 404)
4. Only ONE GET SSE stream per session (second attempt → 409 Conflict)
5. Return SSE stream with server-initiated notifications/requests from child
6. Keep-alive: `: keepalive\n\n` comment every 30s

## DELETE Handler
1. Require Mcp-Session-Id header (not found → 404)
2. Transition session to Closing state
3. Return 200 OK (or 204 No Content) — NOT 202
4. DELETE is idempotent (Closing/Closed returns success)

## Mcp-Session-Id Lifecycle
- Created on initialize response (set in response headers)
- Required on all subsequent requests
- During Closing (drain period): requests return 503 with JSON-RPC -32000
- After Closed (session removed): requests return 404

## Backpressure
Per-session bounded channel of 256 messages. If full, backpressure propagates to child via OS pipe buffer.

## Transport Lifecycle
- Response lifecycle: session counter inc on request accept, dec on res.finish or res.close event
- Transport onclose/onerror: clear session counter, delete transport, kill child
- CORS exposedHeaders includes Mcp-Session-Id (stateful-specific)

## Child Exit / EPIPE
If child exits or stdin EPIPE: mark session failed, close GET SSE stream with error event, pending POST streams get JSON-RPC -32603, return 502 for subsequent requests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST creates new session on initialize (returns Mcp-Session-Id header)
- [ ] POST to existing session: validate session ID, acquire access guard, forward to child
- [ ] POST batch: forward batch to child, return SSE stream with all responses
- [ ] POST notification-only: return 202 immediately
- [ ] POST response correlation: match child responses to correct POST SSE stream by JSON-RPC id
- [ ] Protocol version passthrough: client's protocolVersion forwarded exactly to child
- [ ] Body size limit: 16MB, reject with 413
- [ ] Notification buffering: buffer child notifications before init response, forward after
- [ ] GET SSE stream: one per session, server-initiated messages, 30s keepalive
- [ ] GET SSE stream conflict: second GET returns 409
- [ ] DELETE: return 200/204, transition to Closing. Idempotent
- [ ] Mcp-Session-Id lifecycle: Closing=503, Closed=404
- [ ] Content-Type validation: 415 for non-application/json POST
- [ ] Accept header validation: 406 for missing required Accept values
- [ ] Backpressure: 256 msg bounded channel per session
- [ ] CORS exposedHeaders includes Mcp-Session-Id
- [ ] Response lifecycle: counter inc/dec on request accept/response terminal
- [ ] Child exit / EPIPE: mark failed, close streams, return 502
- [ ] Error responses use GatewayError (correct HTTP status + body format)
- [ ] Max sessions enforced (503)
- [ ] Session timeout with access counting (timeout only when count=0)
- [ ] Health endpoint returns 503 until ready
- [ ] Compatibility harness passes
- [ ] Integration test: full initialize → tool call → response → DELETE lifecycle
- [ ] Integration test: concurrent POSTs to same session
- [ ] Integration test: session timeout fires after idle period

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
notes:
CRITICAL DETAILS FROM PRD:

Supported Protocol Versions: ["2025-06-18", "2025-03-26", "2024-11-05", "2024-10-07"]

Error Response Body Format:
- Transport-level errors (406, 415, 404, 409): plain text body
- Application-level errors (400, 503): JSON-RPC error object {jsonrpc:"2.0", error:{code, message}, id:null}

Body Parsing: Parse JSON body before handler (express.json() equivalent). Unlike SSE mode which reads raw body.

Concurrent POST Response Correlation:
- Multiple POSTs may be in-flight for same session
- Each POST with id gets its own SSE stream response
- Must correlate child stdout responses to the correct POST's SSE stream by matching JSON-RPC id
- Notifications from child go to GET SSE stream (not POST response streams)

title: US-011: Gateway — stdio->Streamable HTTP (Stateless)
description:
Gateway mode: stdio→Streamable HTTP (stateless). Per-request child process with auto-initialization.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync HTTP server
- ChildBridge (US-005) spawned per request
- Auto-init state machine for transparent child initialization
- GatewayError (US-003a) for error responses
- Codec (US-004) for child stdin/stdout
- Logger + Metrics (US-008), Signal handler (US-009)

## File: src/gateway/stdio_to_stateless_http.rs

## Endpoints
- `POST /mcp` (configurable via --streamableHttpPath): JSON-RPC request/notification
- `GET /mcp`: 405 Method Not Allowed
- `DELETE /mcp`: 405 Method Not Allowed
- Health endpoints as configured

## Auto-Init State Machine
Five state variables (per PRD):
- `isInitialized: bool`
- `initializeRequestId: String`
- `isAutoInitializing: bool`
- `pendingOriginalMessages: Vec<RawMessage>` (fixes TS bug D-005)
- `buffer` (for init response capture)

### Case 1: Incoming request IS an initialize request
Forward directly to child (no auto-init). Set isInitialized = true. Return child's response.

### Case 2: Incoming request is NOT initialize (auto-init path)
1. Spawn new ChildBridge
2. Send synthetic initialize request to child (see exact shapes below)
3. If child emits notifications before init response: buffer and forward after init completes
4. Wait for initialize response (capture but do NOT return to client)
5. Send `notifications/initialized` to child
6. Now send the actual client request to child
7. Return child's response to client
8. Kill child after response sent

### Auto-init timeout: 5s (PRD line 225). Kill child + return JSON-RPC -32603.

## Auto-Init Request Shapes
Initialize request:
```json
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": "init_<timestamp_ms>_<random_alphanumeric_9>",
  "method": "initialize",
  "params": {
    "protocolVersion": "<--protocolVersion flag, default 2024-11-05>",
    "capabilities": { "roots": { "listChanged": true }, "sampling": {} },
    "clientInfo": { "name": "supergateway", "version": "<CARGO_PKG_VERSION>" }
  }
}
```

notifications/initialized:
```json
{ "jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "notifications/initialized" }
```

## Error Handling
Wrap entire per-request handler in catch-all. Any unexpected error returns HTTP 500 with JSON-RPC -32603 Internal Error body. This differs from stateful mode which does not have this outer catch.

## Stateless-Specific Behavior
- No Mcp-Session-Id header in any response
- No CORS exposedHeaders for Mcp-Session-Id (no sessions)
- Transport created without sessionIdGenerator equivalent
- Backpressure: per-request (1 message deep), no buffering needed

## Batch POST Handling
Client sends JSON array → batch. Auto-init once per request lifecycle, then forward all. Responses collected as SSE stream events. Notifications-only batch: return 202 after auto-init and forwarding.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST: spawn child, auto-init (or direct forward for initialize), return response, kill child
- [ ] Auto-init Case 1: initialize request forwarded directly, no auto-init
- [ ] Auto-init Case 2: non-init request triggers synthetic initialize, then forward
- [ ] Auto-init timeout: 5s, then kill child + JSON-RPC -32603
- [ ] Child notifications before init response: buffer and forward after init
- [ ] Auto-init state: 5 state variables as specified
- [ ] Outer try/catch: unexpected errors → 500 + JSON-RPC -32603
- [ ] No Mcp-Session-Id in responses, no CORS exposedHeaders for it
- [ ] POST batch: auto-init once, forward all, collect responses as SSE stream
- [ ] POST notification-only: auto-init, forward, return 202
- [ ] GET/DELETE: return 405 Method Not Allowed
- [ ] Content-Type validation: 415 for non-application/json
- [ ] Accept header validation: 406 for missing required values
- [ ] Body size limit: 16MB, reject with 413
- [ ] --streamableHttpPath configurable (default /mcp)
- [ ] --protocolVersion used in auto-init params
- [ ] Child exit during request: return 502, clean up
- [ ] Error responses use GatewayError
- [ ] Max concurrent requests enforced (1024, reject with 503)
- [ ] Pending message buffer: Vec<RawMessage> (fixes TS bug D-005)
- [ ] Health endpoint
- [ ] Compatibility harness passes
- [ ] Integration test: initialize passthrough, tool call, notification
- [ ] Integration test: auto-init timeout
- [ ] Integration test: batch request

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings

title: US-012: Gateway — stdio->SSE
description:
Gateway mode: stdio→SSE. Single shared child process, broadcast to all connected SSE clients.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync HTTP server
- Single ChildBridge (US-005) shared across all clients
- GatewayError (US-003a) for error responses
- Codec (US-004) for child stdin/stdout
- Logger + Metrics (US-008), Signal handler (US-009)

## File: src/gateway/stdio_to_sse.rs

## Endpoints
- `GET <ssePath>` (default /sse): SSE event stream with session tracking
- `POST <messagePath>` (default /message): JSON-RPC message from client to child
- Health endpoints as configured

## SSE Stream
- Response headers: Content-Type: text/event-stream, Cache-Control: no-cache, Connection: keep-alive, X-Accel-Buffering: no
- First event: `event: endpoint\ndata: <baseUrl><messagePath>?sessionId=<uuid>\n\n`
- Subsequent events: `event: message\ndata: <json-rpc-message>\n\n`
- Keep-alive: `: keepalive\n\n` comment every 30s
- Each SSE connection gets a UUID sessionId for POST routing

## Session Tracking
- Each SSE client is a session identified by sessionId (UUID in endpoint URL)
- Sessions tracked in map, cleaned up on disconnect
- `POST <messagePath>?sessionId=<uuid>`: validate sessionId (missing → 400, not found → 503)

## baseUrl Behavior
- `--baseUrl ""` (empty string, default): endpoint URL is relative path
- `--baseUrl "http://example.com"`: endpoint URL is absolute

## Broadcast Pattern
ALL child stdout messages broadcast to ALL connected SSE clients (B-001 in DIVERGENCES.md).

## Backpressure
Per-client bounded mpsc channel with 256 message capacity. If client's channel full, block producer. Blocked >30s → disconnect client, remove from broadcast list. Log at warn level.

## POST Handler
1. Validate sessionId query parameter (missing → 400 Bad Request)
2. Look up session (not found → 503)
3. Read raw body as bytes (NOT through framework body parser — match SDK raw-body behavior)
4. Body size limit: 4MB (matching TS SDK raw-body default). Reject with 413.
5. Validate non-empty body. Empty → 400.
6. Malformed JSON → 400 Bad Request (plain text)
7. Write to child stdin via codec (mutex-protected for concurrent POSTs)
8. Return 202 Accepted always (B-002 in DIVERGENCES.md)

## Child Exit
If shared child process exits, gateway process MUST exit with child's exit code (or 1). This ensures pm2/systemd restart.

## Signal Handling
On signal (SIGTERM/SIGINT), explicitly kill child process group (fixes TS bug D-011 where SSE mode omits cleanup callback).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GET /sse: returns SSE stream with endpoint event (including sessionId), then message events
- [ ] POST /message?sessionId=<uuid>: validate sessionId (missing→400, not found→503), forward to child, return 202
- [ ] POST body read as raw bytes, NOT through framework body parser
- [ ] Body size limit: 4MB (SSE mode), reject with 413
- [ ] Broadcast: all child stdout messages sent to all connected SSE clients
- [ ] Backpressure: 256 msg bounded channel per client, blocked >30s → disconnect
- [ ] SSE response headers: Content-Type, Cache-Control, Connection, X-Accel-Buffering
- [ ] baseUrl: empty string = relative URL, non-empty = absolute URL
- [ ] Malformed JSON POST → 400. Empty body → 400
- [ ] Stdin write serialization: Mutex prevents interleaved writes
- [ ] SSE keep-alive: 30s comment lines
- [ ] Custom headers applied to all responses
- [ ] Session tracking: cleanup on disconnect
- [ ] Dual disconnect detection: request close AND transport close trigger cleanup
- [ ] Child exit → gateway process exit with child's exit code (or 1)
- [ ] Health endpoint returns 503 until child ready
- [ ] Compatibility harness passes
- [ ] Integration test: connect SSE, POST message, receive broadcast response
- [ ] Integration test: rapid connect/disconnect (100+ cycles), no leaked tasks/FDs/stale entries

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings

title: US-013: Gateway — stdio->WebSocket
description:
Gateway mode: stdio→WebSocket. Single shared child process, per-client ID routing via HashMap multiplexer.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync WebSocket server (per US-000 findings)
- Single ChildBridge (US-005) shared across all WS clients
- ID multiplexer: HashMap<MangledId, (ClientId, OriginalId)> (fixes TS bug D-001)
- GatewayError (US-003a), Codec (US-004), Logger + Metrics (US-008), Signal handler (US-009)

## File: src/gateway/stdio_to_ws.rs

## WebSocket Endpoint
- `WS <messagePath>` (default /message): WebSocket upgrade
- Health endpoints on HTTP (not WS) with readiness gating (503 before child+transport ready). Proper if/else with early returns (fixes TS bug D-002).

## ID Multiplexer (Fixes TS Bug D-001)
```rust
struct IdMultiplexer {
    next_id: AtomicU64,
    map: RwLock<HashMap<MangledId, (ClientId, OriginalId)>>,
}
```
On client→child: replace message id with auto-increment MangledId, store mapping.
On child→client: look up MangledId, restore OriginalId, route to correct client. **Remove entry from map after routing** (entries are single-use for request-response pairs).
On client disconnect: remove all correlation entries for that client (stale cleanup).

## Notification/Server-Initiated Message Handling
- Notifications from child (no id): BROADCAST to all clients
- Server-initiated requests from child (messages with id+method, not matching outstanding mangled IDs): broadcast to all clients. Gateway does not interpret — MCP is bidirectional.
- Notifications from client (no id): forward to child without mangling

## Broadcast Without Head-of-Line Blocking
1. Acquire read lock on client map
2. Clone all client channel senders into a local Vec
3. Drop the lock
4. Iterate Vec, try_send to each
NEVER hold RwLock guard across async sends.

## Backpressure
Per-client bounded channel (256 messages). Blocked >30s → disconnect with close frame 1008.

## Stdin Write Serialization
Multiple WS clients send concurrently. All writes to child stdin go through Mutex<ChildStdinWriter>.

## --header Support
Custom headers applied to: WS upgrade response, health endpoint responses, HTTP error responses. Fixes TS omission D-105.

## Child Exit / EPIPE
Close all WebSocket clients with close frame 1011, then **exit gateway process** with child's exit code (or 1). Ensures pm2/systemd restart.

## Max Message Size
16MB per WebSocket message. Reject oversized with close frame 1009 (Message Too Big).

## WebSocket Close Frame Codes
- 1000 Normal Closure: clean shutdown
- 1008 Policy Violation: client too slow (backpressure disconnect)
- 1009 Message Too Big: message exceeds 16MB
- 1011 Unexpected Condition: child died / EPIPE

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WS endpoint on messagePath, accepts WebSocket upgrade
- [ ] ID multiplexer: HashMap routing, auto-increment MangledId, OriginalId preserved
- [ ] Client→child: mangle id, forward via codec
- [ ] Child→client: unmangle id, route to correct client, remove entry from map
- [ ] Stale ID cleanup: remove correlation entries on client disconnect
- [ ] Notifications (no id): broadcast to ALL clients
- [ ] Server-initiated requests from child: broadcast to all clients
- [ ] Broadcast: clone senders, drop lock, then send (no HOL blocking)
- [ ] Backpressure: 256 msg bounded channel, 30s timeout, disconnect slow clients
- [ ] WS close frames: 1000 (normal), 1008 (policy), 1009 (too big), 1011 (unexpected)
- [ ] Max message size: 16MB, reject with 1009
- [ ] Stdin serialization: Mutex prevents interleaved writes
- [ ] --header applied to WS upgrade, health, and error responses
- [ ] Child exit / EPIPE: close all clients with 1011, then exit gateway process
- [ ] Client disconnect: remove from map, prompt non-blocking cleanup
- [ ] Health endpoint (HTTP): readiness gating, proper early returns (D-002)
- [ ] Compatibility harness passes (with divergence flag for ID handling per D-001)
- [ ] Integration test: two clients, send request, verify correct routing
- [ ] Integration test: notification + server-initiated request broadcast
- [ ] Integration test: slow client disconnected after backpressure timeout
- [ ] Integration test: rapid connect/disconnect (100+), no leaked tasks/routing entries/FDs

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
notes:
CRITICAL: Broadcast Without Head-of-Line Blocking pattern:
1. Acquire read lock on client map
2. Clone all client channel senders into a local Vec
3. Drop the lock
4. Iterate Vec and send to each (try_send or per-client spawned forwarder)
NEVER hold RwLock guard across async sends — causes deadlocks.

Backpressure: Per-client bounded channel (256 msgs). Blocked >30s → disconnect client.

Stdin write serialization: Multiple WS clients may send concurrently.
All writes to shared child stdin MUST go through a Mutex to prevent interleaved JSON lines.

title: US-014: HTTP Client Stack
description:
HTTP/1.1 client for connecting to remote MCP servers. Used by client modes (SSE→stdio, HTTP→stdio).

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync HTTP client (per US-000 findings), else hyper or minimal HTTP/1.1 client
- TLS via rustls (for HTTPS URLs — bead-level scope addition, not in PRD but needed for real servers)
- Reuses --header and --oauth2Bearer for outgoing requests

## File: src/client/http.rs

## Core API
```rust
struct HttpClient {
    headers: Vec<(String, String)>,
    pool: ConnectionPool,  // max 4 idle per host
}

impl HttpClient {
    async fn post(&self, url: &str, body: &[u8], content_type: &str)
        -> Result<HttpResponse, HttpClientError>;
    async fn get_stream(&self, url: &str, accept: &str)
        -> Result<HttpStreamResponse, HttpClientError>;
    async fn delete(&self, url: &str) -> Result<HttpResponse, HttpClientError>;
}
```

## Connection Pooling
Connection keep-alive with bounded pool: max 4 idle connections per host.

## Timeout Matrix
- Connect: 10s
- Request: 30s
- Idle: 60s

## Retry and Redirect Policy
- No automatic retry of non-idempotent JSON-RPC POSTs
- Redirects: follow up to 5 redirects for GET, do NOT follow for POST

## Content-Type Detection
Response Content-Type MUST be exposed to callers. Critical for US-017 branching:
- `text/event-stream` → parse as SSE stream (reuse US-015)
- `application/json` → parse as single JSON-RPC response

## Mcp-Session-Id Header Support
API must support capturing Mcp-Session-Id from response headers and injecting on subsequent requests (for US-017 stateful session management).

## Custom Headers
All headers from --header applied to every outgoing request. --oauth2Bearer adds `Authorization: Bearer <token>` only when provided with non-empty value (fixes TS bug D-003).

## Error Response Body Parsing
On non-2xx responses:
- Content-Type application/json: parse as JSON-RPC error, propagate
- Otherwise: wrap status + body text in HttpClientError

title: US-015: SSE Client (EventSource)
description:
SSE client (EventSource) for connecting to remote SSE-based MCP servers.

## Tech Stack
- Built on HttpClient (US-014) GET stream
- SSE event parsing per W3C EventSource spec

## File: src/client/sse.rs

## SSE Event Parsing
Parse `text/event-stream` format:
- `event: <type>\n` (default: "message")
- `data: <payload>\n` (may span multiple lines, concatenated with \n)
- `id: <id>\n` (last event ID for reconnection)
- `: comment\n` (ignored, used for keepalive)
- Blank line terminates event

## Endpoint URL Discovery
The SSE server sends the POST endpoint URL in the first event:
- `event: endpoint\ndata: <url>\n\n`
- May be relative (resolve against server base URL) or absolute
- Client must wait for endpoint event before sending any messages

## Custom Headers on SSE Connection
Custom headers (--header and --oauth2Bearer) must be applied to the SSE GET connection request itself, not just to POST requests. In TS, this is done via a custom fetch wrapper in eventSourceInit. In Rust, inject headers into the GET request that establishes the SSE stream.

## Reconnection
If SSE connection drops:
- Reconnect with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, max 30s)
- Include `Last-Event-ID` header if available
- Auth/custom headers preserved on reconnect
- Server may send `retry:` field to override reconnect interval
- Log reconnection attempts at warn level

## Disconnect Buffering
During disconnect (while reconnecting):
- Buffer stdin input up to 256 messages
- Reject with error if buffer full
- Flush buffered messages after successful reconnect

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parse SSE events: event type, data (multi-line), id, comments
- [ ] Endpoint URL discovery: capture endpoint event, resolve relative URLs
- [ ] Custom headers applied to SSE GET connection (not just POST)
- [ ] Keepalive comment lines: silently consumed
- [ ] Reconnection: exponential backoff with Last-Event-ID
- [ ] Auth/custom headers preserved on reconnect
- [ ] retry: field respected
- [ ] Disconnect buffering: up to 256 messages, reject if full
- [ ] Yield parsed events as async stream
- [ ] Test: connect to SSE server, parse events
- [ ] Test: multi-line data events concatenated correctly
- [ ] Test: endpoint URL discovery (relative and absolute)
- [ ] Test: reconnection with Last-Event-ID
- [ ] Test: disconnect buffering (fill to 256, verify rejection at 257)

- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings

title: US-016: Gateway — SSE->stdio
description:
Gateway mode: SSE→stdio. Client mode that bridges stdin to remote SSE-based MCP server.

## Tech Stack
- SSE Client (US-015) for remote connection
- HttpClient (US-014) for POST requests
- Codec (US-004) for stdin/stdout line-delimited IO
- Logger (US-008), Signal handler (US-009)

## File: src/gateway/sse_to_stdio.rs

## Client Lifecycle
Module-level singleton client using `Arc<Mutex<Option<Client>>>` or equivalent safe pattern.

## Init Dance (THREE cases)
1. **First stdin message IS initialize**: create MCP client with caller's clientInfo and capabilities. Intercept initialize to capture response + do protocol version passthrough.
2. **First stdin message is NOT initialize**: create fallback client with default info, connect immediately, then forward the original message. Do NOT crash (fixes TS bug D-004).
3. **Protocol version passthrough**: intercept initialize request to remote, replace protocolVersion with value from caller's original stdin initialize message (clean interceptor, not monkey-patch).

### Init-Result-Capture Pattern
1. Connect SSE transport, receive endpoint URL
2. First stdin message: branch on case 1 or 2 above
3. POST initialize to endpoint URL
4. Capture initialize response from SSE stream
5. Write initialize response to stdout
6. POST notifications/initialized to endpoint URL
7. Enter normal bidirectional message loop
Do NOT double-send initialize.

## Normal Message Loop
- Read JSON-RPC messages from stdin (line-delimited via codec)
- POST each message to endpoint URL via HttpClient
- Receive responses/notifications via SSE stream
- Write all to stdout (line-delimited)

## Message Forwarding Rules
- Non-request message forwarding: ALL inbound SSE messages (notifications, server-initiated requests) forwarded to stdout in arrival order without interpretation
- Server-initiated requests from remote: forward to stdout without interpretation
- Error responses from remote: write as JSON-RPC error objects to stdout with correct code and message
- Error prefix stripping: remove `MCP error <code>:` prefix from error messages before forwarding to stdout

## SSE Reconnection
Uses US-015 reconnection (exponential backoff, Last-Event-ID, 256 msg buffer during disconnect).

## Log Direction Labels (Fixes TS Bug D-006)
- Stdin→Remote: `"Stdin → SSE:"`
- Remote→Stdout: `"SSE → Stdout:"`

## Exit Behavior
- Remote transport close → exit(1) (for pm2/systemd restart)
- Remote transport error → log only, do NOT exit (B-004 in DIVERGENCES.md)
- stdin EOF → graceful shutdown (close SSE connection, exit cleanly)
- Signal (SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP) → graceful shutdown

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Connect to --sse URL, wait for endpoint event
- [ ] Init dance case 1: first message is initialize → client with caller's info, protocol version passthrough
- [ ] Init dance case 2: first message is NOT initialize → fallback client, connect, forward message
- [ ] Init-result-capture: capture initialize response during connect, write to stdout
- [ ] Non-request message forwarding: all inbound messages forwarded to stdout in order
- [ ] Server-initiated requests: forwarded without interpretation
- [ ] Error responses: written as JSON-RPC errors to stdout
- [ ] Error prefix stripping: remove `MCP error <code>:` prefix
- [ ] Read stdin line-by-line, POST to endpoint URL
- [ ] SSE reconnection: buffer messages (256), flush on reconnect, exit on failure
- [ ] Correct log direction labels (Stdin → SSE, SSE → Stdout)
- [ ] Remote close: exit(1). Remote error: log only
- [ ] stdin EOF: graceful shutdown
- [ ] Custom headers applied to outgoing requests
- [ ] Integration test: connect to mock SSE server, send request, receive response
- [ ] Test: init dance case 1 (initialize first) and case 2 (non-init first)
- [ ] Test: error prefix stripping works
- [ ] Test: reconnection buffers and flushes messages

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
notes:
Init-result-capture and exit behavior patterns shared with US-017 (HTTP→stdio).
Same --header / --oauth2Bearer header injection applies.

title: US-017: Gateway — Streamable HTTP->stdio
description:
Gateway mode: Streamable HTTP→stdio. Client mode that bridges stdin to remote Streamable HTTP MCP server.

## Tech Stack
- HttpClient (US-014) for POST/GET/DELETE requests
- SSE Client (US-015) for parsing SSE stream responses
- Codec (US-004) for stdin/stdout line-delimited IO
- Logger (US-008), Signal handler (US-009)

## File: src/gateway/http_to_stdio.rs

## Client Lifecycle
Module-level singleton client using `Arc<Mutex<Option<Client>>>` or equivalent safe pattern.

## Init Dance (THREE cases — same as US-016)
1. **First stdin message IS initialize**: create client with caller's clientInfo/capabilities, protocol version passthrough
2. **First stdin message is NOT initialize**: create fallback client with default info, connect immediately, forward original message
3. **Protocol version passthrough**: intercept initialize request, replace protocolVersion with caller's value

### Init-Result-Capture Pattern
1. First stdin message: branch on case 1 or 2
2. POST initialize to server URL
3. Capture initialize response (may be SSE stream or JSON — check Content-Type)
4. Write initialize response to stdout
5. POST notifications/initialized
6. Enter normal bidirectional message loop

## Content-Type Response Branching
POST responses from Streamable HTTP servers may be:
- `application/json` → single JSON-RPC response, write to stdout
- `text/event-stream` → SSE stream with multiple events, parse each, write data to stdout
Client MUST check Content-Type and branch accordingly (uses HttpClient Content-Type from US-014).

## Server-Initiated GET SSE Stream
After initialization, open a GET request to the server URL with:
- `Accept: text/event-stream`
- `Mcp-Session-Id` header (from initialize response)
This receives server-initiated notifications/requests. Write each event's data to stdout.

Note: Unlike SSE→stdio mode, HTTP→stdio does NOT use custom fetch for the GET SSE stream. Custom headers (--header, --oauth2Bearer) applied via requestInit.headers on POST requests only.

## Message Forwarding Rules
- Non-request message forwarding: ALL inbound messages forwarded to stdout in arrival order without interpretation
- Server-initiated requests: forwarded without interpretation
- Error responses from remote: write as JSON-RPC error objects to stdout
- Error prefix stripping: remove `MCP error <code>:` prefix from error messages before forwarding

## Log Direction Labels
- Stdin→Remote: `"Stdin → HTTP:"`
- Remote→Stdout: `"HTTP → Stdout:"`

## Exit Behavior
- Remote transport close → exit(1)
- Remote transport error → log only
- stdin EOF → graceful shutdown
- Signal → graceful shutdown

title: US-003a: GatewayError Type and HTTP Response Mapping
description:
Shared error type hierarchy used by all gateways. Implements Asupersync IntoResponse trait to convert errors to correct HTTP status + JSON-RPC error body.

## File: src/error.rs (new file)

## Error Type
```rust
#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
enum GatewayError {
    // Transport-level errors (plain text response body)
    #[error("bad request: {0}")]
    BadRequest(String),                      // 400 (malformed JSON — NOT valid JSON-RPC, pure HTTP error)
    #[error("not acceptable: {0}")]
    NotAcceptable(String),                   // 406
    #[error("unsupported media type: {0}")]
    UnsupportedMediaType(String),            // 415
    #[error("session not found")]
    SessionNotFound,                         // 404
    #[error("conflict: {0}")]
    Conflict(String),                        // 409
    #[error("method not allowed")]
    MethodNotAllowed,                        // 405
    #[error("payload too large")]
    PayloadTooLarge,                         // 413
    #[error("request header fields too large")]
    HeaderTooLarge,                          // 431
    #[error("service unavailable: {0}")]
    ServiceUnavailable(String),              // 503 (max sessions, not ready)

    // Application-level errors (JSON-RPC error response body)
    #[error("invalid request: {0}")]
    InvalidRequest(String),                  // 200 + JSON-RPC -32600
    #[error("session closing")]
    SessionClosing,                          // 503 + JSON-RPC -32000
    #[error("child process dead")]
    ChildDead,                               // 502 + JSON-RPC -32603
    #[error("auto-init timeout")]
    AutoInitTimeout,                         // 502 + JSON-RPC -32603
    #[error("internal error: {0}")]
    Internal(String),                        // 500 + JSON-RPC -32603

    // Wrapped errors
    #[error("child error: {0}")]
    Child(#[from] ChildError),
    #[error("session error: {0}")]
    Session(SessionError),
    #[error("I/O error: {0}")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
}
```

IMPORTANT: `InvalidRequest` returns HTTP **200** (NOT 400). Per JSON-RPC 2.0 spec — when JSON is valid but not valid JSON-RPC, the error is application-level, not transport-level. The HTTP request itself succeeded.

IMPORTANT: `BadRequest` (malformed JSON) returns HTTP 400 with **plain text** body (NOT JSON-RPC). Malformed JSON cannot be valid JSON-RPC, so it's a pure HTTP error.

Stub types for `ChildError` and `SessionError`: define in error.rs as `pub struct ChildError(pub String)` and `pub struct SessionError(pub String)`. These stubs will be replaced by concrete types from US-005 (child.rs) and US-006 (session.rs).

The IntoResponse trait depends on Asupersync web framework API validated in US-000. Adapt trait name/path per spike/FINDINGS.md if needed.

## Complete Error Mapping Table (from PRD)
| Condition | HTTP Status | JSON-RPC Code | Response Body |
|---|---|---|---|
| Malformed JSON body | 400 | — | Plain text |
| Valid JSON, invalid JSON-RPC | 200 | -32600 | JSON-RPC error |
| Missing Accept headers (StreamableHTTP) | 406 | — | Plain text |
| Wrong Content-Type (StreamableHTTP) | 415 | — | Plain text |
| No session / invalid session (stateful POST) | 404 | — | Plain text |
| Session closing/closed | 503 | -32000 | JSON-RPC error |
| Child process dead | 502 | -32603 | JSON-RPC error |
| Auto-init timeout | 502 | -32603 | JSON-RPC error |
| Internal error (stateless catch-all) | 500 | -32603 | JSON-RPC error |
| Message too large | 413 | — | Plain text |
| Header too large | 431 | — | Plain text |
| Max sessions reached | 503 | — | Plain text |
| GET/DELETE on stateless | 405 | — | Plain text |
| GET SSE stream already open | 409 | — | Plain text |
| Not ready (health) | 503 | — | Plain text |
| CORS preflight | 204 | — | Empty (not an error) |
| Notification-only POST | 202 | — | Empty (not an error) |

Note: Last two rows are not error variants — included for mapping completeness. Handled by normal response paths.

## IntoResponse Implementation
For errors WITH JSON-RPC codes:
```json
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","error":{"code":<code>,"message":"<message>"},"id":null}
```
Content-Type: application/json

For errors WITHOUT JSON-RPC codes: plain text error message. Content-Type: text/plain

## Helper Constructors
```rust
fn bad_request() -> Self            // 400 plain text (malformed JSON)
fn invalid_request(msg: &str) -> Self // 200 + JSON-RPC -32600
fn invalid_session() -> Self        // 404
fn session_closing() -> Self        // 503 + JSON-RPC -32000
fn child_dead() -> Self             // 502 + JSON-RPC -32603
fn max_sessions() -> Self           // 503 plain text
fn not_ready() -> Self              // 503 plain text
fn missing_accept() -> Self         // 406
fn wrong_content_type(expected: &str, got: &str) -> Self // 415
fn payload_too_large() -> Self      // 413
fn header_too_large() -> Self       // 431
fn method_not_allowed() -> Self     // 405
fn sse_stream_conflict() -> Self    // 409
fn auto_init_timeout() -> Self      // 502 + JSON-RPC -32603
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GatewayError enum with all variants from the error mapping table
- [ ] IntoResponse trait implementation mapping each variant to correct HTTP status
- [ ] JSON-RPC error response body for variants with JSON-RPC codes (Content-Type: application/json)
- [ ] Plain text response body for transport-level errors (Content-Type: text/plain)
- [ ] InvalidRequest returns HTTP 200 (not 400) — per JSON-RPC 2.0 spec
- [ ] BadRequest (malformed JSON) returns 400 with plain text (not JSON-RPC)
- [ ] All helper constructors listed above
- [ ] Stub types for ChildError and SessionError
- [ ] Unit tests: each error variant → correct HTTP status + correct body format
- [ ] Test: InvalidRequest → HTTP 200 + JSON-RPC error body
- [ ] Test: BadRequest → HTTP 400 + plain text body

- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass

title: US-002: Project Scaffolding and CLI
description:
Create the Rust project structure with Cargo.toml, toolchain pin, and complete CLI argument parsing using clap derive macros.

## Tech Stack
- Rust nightly (pinned in rust-toolchain.toml, e.g. nightly-2026-03-01)
- `asupersync = "=0.2.7"` (exact pin)
- `clap` (derive) for CLI parsing
- Dependencies: asupersync, clap, serde, serde_json, uuid, nix, regex

## File Structure
```
Cargo.toml
rust-toolchain.toml
src/
  main.rs
  cli.rs
```

## CLI Flags (Complete)
| Flag | Type | Default | Scope |
|------|------|---------|-------|
| `--stdio <cmd>` | string | — | Input |
| `--sse <url>` | string | — | Input |
| `--streamableHttp <url>` | string | — | Input |
| `--outputTransport` | choice | mode-dependent | Output: stdio|sse|ws|streamableHttp |
| `--port` | number | 8000 | Server modes |
| `--baseUrl` | string | '' | stdio->SSE |
| `--ssePath` | string | /sse | stdio->SSE |
| `--messagePath` | string | /message | stdio->SSE, stdio->WS |
| `--streamableHttpPath` | string | /mcp | stdio->StreamableHTTP |
| `--logLevel` | choice | info | All: debug|info|none |
| `--cors [origins]` | array | — | Server modes |
| `--healthEndpoint [eps]` | array | [] | Server modes |
| `--header [headers]` | array | [] | All |
| `--oauth2Bearer <token>` | string | — | All |
| `--stateful` | boolean | false | stdio->StreamableHTTP |
| `--sessionTimeout <ms>` | number | — | Stateful HTTP only |
| `--protocolVersion` | string | 2024-11-05 | Stateless HTTP only |

## Default outputTransport Logic
- If `--sse` or `--streamableHttp` input: default to `stdio`
- If `--stdio` input: default to `sse`
- Use clap-parsed input mode to determine (cleaner than TS raw argv scan)

## Valid Transport Combinations
Only these input→output pairs are valid:
| Input | Valid Outputs |
|-------|--------------|
| `--stdio` | sse, ws, streamableHttp |
| `--sse` | stdio |
| `--streamableHttp` | stdio |

Reject invalid combinations at CLI parse time with clear error message.

## --header Parsing Rules
Format: `--header "Name: Value"`. Split on first `:` only (value may contain `:`).
Trim whitespace from both name and value. Reject headers without `:` separator.
Multiple `--header` flags accumulate into a Vec.

## --cors Behavior
- `--cors` absent: CORS disabled entirely (no CORS headers on any response)
- `--cors` present with no values: CORS enabled with `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`
- `--cors origin1 origin2`: CORS enabled with specific origins (reflect Origin if in list)

## --healthEndpoint Behavior
- `--healthEndpoint` absent: no health endpoint
- `--healthEndpoint /healthz`: single health endpoint at /healthz
- `--healthEndpoint /healthz /ready`: multiple health endpoints (both paths serve health)

## --oauth2Bearer Behavior
Only add `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header if flag is actually provided with a non-empty value.
Fixes TS bug D-003 where yargs always includes the key, producing "Bearer undefined".

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cargo.toml with all dependencies, `rust-toolchain.toml` (pinned nightly)
- [ ] clap struct with ALL flags per CLI Flags table. Mode-dependent defaults for `--outputTransport`
- [ ] Mutual exclusivity: exactly one of `--stdio`/`--sse`/`--streamableHttp`
- [ ] Valid transport combinations enforced (see table above)
- [ ] Path flags validated to start with `/`
- [ ] `--sessionTimeout` validated positive
- [ ] `--header` parsing: split on first `:`, trim, reject malformed
- [ ] `--cors` three-state behavior (absent / present-no-values / with-origins)
- [ ] `--healthEndpoint` multi-value support
- [ ] `--oauth2Bearer` only adds header when actually provided with value
- [ ] `--help` output lists all flags with camelCase names and mode-dependent defaults
- [ ] Exit codes: 0 success, 1 error (match TS)
- [ ] `cargo +nightly build --release` succeeds

## Quality Gates

title: US-003: JSON-RPC 2.0 Types
description:
Implement JSON-RPC 2.0 message types with serde_json::RawValue for opaque pass-through. This is the foundational protocol layer used by all gateways.

## Tech Stack
- serde + serde_json with RawValue
- IMPORTANT: serde_json::RawValue is a DST (like str). MUST use Box<RawValue> for owned values

## File: src/jsonrpc.rs

## Core Type: RawMessage
A flat struct is the wire type — NOT an enum. The same struct holds requests, responses, notifications, and errors. Classify via helper methods.

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct RawMessage {
    jsonrpc: String,                        // always "2.0"
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    id: Option<Box<RawValue>>,              // number, string, or null — preserved exactly
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    method: Option<String>,                 // present for requests/notifications
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    params: Option<Box<RawValue>>,          // opaque
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    result: Option<Box<RawValue>>,          // opaque
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    error: Option<Box<RawValue>>,           // opaque (contains code, message, data)
}
```

IMPORTANT: Do NOT use `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`. JSON-RPC messages may contain extension fields. Unknown fields must be silently preserved via `#[serde(flatten)] extra: HashMap<String, RawValue>` or simply dropped (dropping is acceptable for a transparent proxy).

## id Field Semantics
- `id: None` (field absent) → notification (no response expected)
- `id: Some(RawValue("null"))` → valid JSON-RPC id of null (response expected, response id will also be null)
- `id: Some(RawValue("42"))` → numeric id
- `id: Some(RawValue("\"abc\""))` → string id
Must distinguish None (absent) from Some(null). Use `#[serde(default, deserialize_with = "deserialize_optional_raw")]` or similar.

## Classification Helpers
```rust
fn is_request(&self) -> bool       // has id + method
fn is_response(&self) -> bool      // has id + (result or error), no method
fn is_notification(&self) -> bool  // has method, no id (id field absent, NOT id:null)
fn is_initialize_request(&self) -> bool // method == "initialize" && is_request()
fn method_str(&self) -> Option<&str> // convenience accessor
```

## Error Response Construction
Helper to build JSON-RPC error responses:
```rust
fn error_response(id: Option<Box<RawValue>>, code: i32, message: &str) -> RawMessage
```
Used by GatewayError (US-003a) and auto-init (US-011) to construct error bodies.
If id is None (notification that errored), use `RawValue::from_string("null")` for the response id.

## Batch Support
Batch = JSON array at top level. Deserialization strategy:
1. Try `serde_json::from_str::<RawMessage>(line)` first (common case, single message)
2. If that fails with "expected struct", try `serde_json::from_str::<Vec<RawMessage>>(line)`
3. If both fail → malformed JSON (log + discard for stdio, 400 for HTTP)
Alternatively: peek first non-whitespace byte. `{` → single, `[` → batch. This avoids double-parse.
Forward batch to child as a single line (re-serialize Vec<RawMessage> to one JSON line).

## Protocol Transparency Rules
- Unknown methods: forward without interpretation
- Arbitrary params/result shapes: forward as-is
- Arbitrary error.data: forward as-is
- Batch JSON-RPC requests (JSON arrays): forward to child as single line
- JSON-RPC notifications (no id): forward without awaiting response
- Server-initiated requests: forward from child/remote to client without interpretation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `RawMessage` struct (NOT enum) using `Box<RawValue>` for opaque pass-through of params/result/error
- [ ] id field: correctly distinguishes absent (None) vs null (Some(null)) vs value (Some(42), Some("abc"))
- [ ] `is_request()`, `is_response()`, `is_notification()`, `is_initialize_request()` helpers
- [ ] `error_response(id, code, message)` constructor helper
- [ ] No `deny_unknown_fields` — silently accept extension fields
- [ ] Batch support: detect and deserialize JSON arrays as `Vec<RawMessage>`
- [ ] Unknown methods: deserialize without rejection
- [ ] Round-trip tests: request, response, notification, error, batch, null id, string id, numeric id, unknown method, large params
- [ ] Test: absent id vs explicit null id are distinct (`is_notification()` returns true only for absent)
- [ ] Test: error_response() produces valid JSON-RPC error with correct structure

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
notes:
PRD inconsistency: US-003 acceptance criterion mentions "JsonRpcMessage enum" but the PRD architecture section (Core Types) specifies RawMessage as a flat struct. This bead follows the architecture section — RawMessage struct with classification helpers, NOT an enum.

Standard JSON-RPC error code constants to define:
  PARSE_ERROR = -32700
  INVALID_REQUEST = -32600
  METHOD_NOT_FOUND = -32601
  INTERNAL_ERROR = -32603
  SERVER_ERROR = -32000
These are used by GatewayError (US-003a) and other modules.

Unknown fields: use #[serde(flatten)] extra: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> to preserve extension fields for protocol transparency. Do NOT drop unknown fields — that breaks round-trip fidelity.

title: US-004: Line-Delimited Stdio Codec
description:
Line-delimited codec for reading/writing JSON-RPC messages over stdin/stdout pipes.

## Tech Stack
- AsyncBufRead/AsyncWrite from asupersync (or std BufReader wrapper)
- serde_json for parsing

## File: src/codec.rs

## Read Path (stdout)
1. Read lines from AsyncBufRead (newline-delimited)
2. Line separator: accept BOTH \n AND \r\n (Windows compat). Split on \n, strip trailing \r
3. Skip empty lines (whitespace-only)
4. Per-message size limit: 16MB. If a single line exceeds 16MB, log error and skip (do not crash)
5. Validate UTF-8: invalid UTF-8 on stdout is a FATAL protocol violation. Return error to caller (caller kills child/session, returns JSON-RPC -32603 to in-flight requests). Increment decode_errors counter. Do NOT use lossy decode for stdout.
6. Parse each line as JSON via serde_json::from_str::<RawMessage>
7. On parse failure: log at error level with first 200 chars of line, skip message
8. Partial line buffer limit: 64MB max (from PRD). If partial buffer exceeds 64MB, treat as protocol violation — kill child/session
9. Handle partial UTF-8 at chunk boundaries correctly (multi-byte codepoints split across reads must be buffered, not treated as invalid)

## Read Path (stderr) — Lossy Mode
Separate read function for stderr with lossy UTF-8 decode:
- Replace invalid UTF-8 bytes with U+FFFD (lossy decode)
- Never return fatal error for invalid UTF-8 on stderr
- Used by ChildBridge (US-005) for stderr reader task

## Write Path
1. Serialize RawMessage to JSON string (serde_json::to_string)
2. Append \n
3. Write to AsyncWrite
4. Flush after each message (critical for pipe buffering — child needs messages immediately)
5. Mutex-protected for shared-child modes (SSE, WS, stateful HTTP). The caller or a write wrapper must hold a mutex to prevent interleaved writes from concurrent request handlers.

## EOF Handling
If trailing buffer after EOF is valid JSON, emit as final frame. If invalid, log warning and discard.

## Global Memory Budget
The 64MB partial buffer limit is per-child. There is a 256MB global cap across all sessions (see US-005 notes). The codec itself enforces the per-child 64MB limit. The global semaphore is owned by ChildBridge (US-005) and passed in at construction.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `read_message()`: read one line, parse as RawMessage, return
- [ ] `write_message()`: serialize + newline + flush, mutex-protected for shared-child modes
- [ ] Line separator: handles both \n and \r\n (strip \r before JSON parse)
- [ ] Empty lines skipped
- [ ] Stdout invalid UTF-8: FATAL error returned to caller (not lossy decode). Caller kills child/session
- [ ] Stderr lossy decode mode: replace invalid bytes with U+FFFD, never fatal
- [ ] Per-message size limit: 16MB max per line. Lines exceeding limit are logged and skipped
- [ ] Partial buffer limit: 64MB max. Exceeded → return fatal error (caller kills child)
- [ ] Partial UTF-8 at chunk boundaries: buffered correctly (not treated as invalid)
- [ ] Parse errors: logged at error level with truncated preview, message skipped
- [ ] EOF handling: trailing valid JSON emitted as final frame; invalid trailing data logged and discarded
- [ ] Batch support: JSON array lines forwarded as-is (parsed as Vec<RawMessage> by caller, not codec)
- [ ] Unit tests: single message, batch, empty lines, \r\n, oversized message, parse error, partial buffer overflow, invalid UTF-8 on stdout (fatal), invalid UTF-8 on stderr (lossy), EOF with/without trailing newline
- [ ] criterion benchmark: parse 10k messages (criterion microbenchmark framework)

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
notes:
IMPORTANT: Line separator must accept BOTH 
 AND 
 for Windows compatibility.
Split on 
, then strip trailing  from each line before JSON parsing.

title: US-005: Child Process Manager (ChildBridge)
description:
ChildBridge: spawn child MCP servers via `sh -c`, manage process groups with setsid/killpg, handle stdin/stdout/stderr as separate async tasks.

## Tech Stack
- Use `asupersync::process::Command` if available (per US-000 findings), else `std::process::Command` with async wrapper
- `nix` crate for setsid/killpg/waitpid
- Spawned inside Asupersync Region — Region cancel kills child

## File: src/child.rs

## Spawn Sequence
1. `Command::new("sh").arg("-c").arg(stdio_cmd)` with `pre_exec(|| { setsid(); Ok(()) })`
2. `stdin(Stdio::piped())`, `stdout(Stdio::piped())`, `stderr(Stdio::piped())`
3. `.take()` stdin, stdout, stderr into separate async tasks

## Stdout Channel Design (CRITICAL for deadlock prevention)
Stdout reader task writes to a bounded async channel (capacity 256 messages).
If channel is full (child producing faster than gateway consuming):
1. Log warning at debug level
2. Block the stdout reader task (natural backpressure to child via pipe buffer)
3. Do NOT drop messages
Gateway consumer tasks read from the receiver end. On child exit, stdout reader closes the sender.

## Stdout Invalid UTF-8 — FATAL
Invalid UTF-8 on stdout is a FATAL protocol violation (PRD line 335).
- Signal error to owning session/gateway
- Session/gateway must: close session, return JSON-RPC -32603 to in-flight requests
- Log the decode error, increment decode_errors counter
- Do NOT use lossy decode for stdout

## Kill Sequence
1. SIGTERM to process group (`killpg(pgid, SIGTERM)`)
2. Wait up to 5s for child exit
3. Check `waitpid(child_pid, WNOHANG)` before SIGKILL (PID reuse safety)
4. If still alive: SIGKILL to process group
5. Drain remaining stdout/stderr (up to 1MB, then truncate with log warning)
6. Close all FDs (stdin/stdout/stderr handles from .take() must be explicitly dropped)

## EPIPE Handling
- On broken stdin pipe: mark child as dead immediately, stop accepting new messages
- Propagate error to caller with transport-appropriate status (HTTP 502, WS close, SSE error event)
- Stateful sessions with dead child are terminally failed (no auto-restart — MCP session state lost)

## Stderr Handling
- Captured and logged at error level (all modes consistently)
- Buffer limit: 64KB per read. Lines >4KB truncated with `[truncated]` suffix
- Never queued — logged inline by stderr reader task (no channel, no buffering beyond per-read 64KB)
- Invalid UTF-8: lossy decode (replace with U+FFFD), do not crash (uses codec stderr lossy mode)

## Grandchildren
- `setsid` creates new process group. `killpg` sends signal to entire group
- Handles TS bug where `child.kill()` only kills `sh`, leaving `npm`/`node` orphaned

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Spawn via `sh -c` with `pre_exec(setsid)` for process group
- [ ] Use asupersync process Command if available, else std::process::Command with async wrapper
- [ ] Stdin/stdout `.take()`-ed into separate async tasks
- [ ] Stdout reader writes to bounded channel (256 capacity) — blocks on full, does not drop
- [ ] Stdout invalid UTF-8: FATAL error signaled to owning session/gateway (not lossy decode)
- [ ] `kill()`: SIGTERM to pgid -> 5s wait -> SIGKILL (with PID reuse safety check via waitpid WNOHANG)
- [ ] `Drop` impl sends SIGTERM to pgid (safety net)
- [ ] EPIPE detection: on broken stdin pipe, mark child dead, propagate error to caller
- [ ] Child exit detection: detect via wait status, propagate to owning session/gateway
- [ ] Stderr: read + log at error level, 64KB buffer, 4KB line truncation, lossy UTF-8 decode
- [ ] Spawned inside Region — Region cancel kills child
- [ ] Shutdown drain: up to 1MB stdout/stderr after kill, then truncate with log warning
- [ ] Integration test: spawn, read message, cancel Region, verify pgid killed within 2s
- [ ] Integration test: write to dead child stdin, verify EPIPE handling
- [ ] Integration test: rapid spawn/kill cycles (100+), verify no leaked pids or fds

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
notes:
IMPORTANT: Global memory semaphore — 64MB max partial buffer per child, 256MB global cap across ALL sessions.
Implement as Arc<Semaphore> passed to each ChildBridge on spawn. New sessions rejected (503) when budget exhausted.
This prevents worst case 64MB × 1024 sessions = 64GB memory exhaustion.

PID 1 zombie reaping: NOT owned by this bead. Zombie reaping (SIGCHLD handler with waitpid(-1, WNOHANG)) is owned by US-009 (Signal Handling). ChildBridge only reaps its OWN child via direct waitpid(child_pid).

title: US-006: Session Manager
description:
Session manager for stateful Streamable HTTP mode. Manages session lifecycle, access counting, and timeout.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync Region per session (Region cancel cascades to ChildBridge cleanup)
- DashMap or RwLock<HashMap> for session storage
- UUID v4 for session IDs (Mcp-Session-Id header) — cryptographically random, unguessable

## File: src/session.rs

## Session State Machine
```
(none) → Active → Closing → Closed
                    ↑
             (DELETE or timeout)
```
- Active: child alive, accepting requests
- Closing: received DELETE or timeout fired. Requests rejected with 503 (NOT 404). 5s drain timeout for in-flight requests, then Closed
- Closed: session removed from map, Region cancelled, child killed. Subsequent requests return 404 (session not found)

Note: A transient "Created" state may exist between session map insertion and child spawn completion. If child spawn fails, session transitions directly to Closed.

## Closing→Closed Drain Timeout
When entering Closing state:
1. Stop accepting NEW requests (return 503 with JSON-RPC -32000 for this session)
2. Allow in-flight requests up to 5s to complete
3. After 5s or all in-flight requests complete (whichever first), transition to Closed
4. Closed: cancel Region, kill child, remove from session map. Subsequent requests return 404.

## Access Counting (RAII Guard)
```rust
struct SessionAccessGuard { session_id: String, counter: Arc<AtomicU64> }
impl Drop for SessionAccessGuard { fn drop(&mut self) { self.counter.fetch_sub(1, Relaxed); } }
```
- Increment on request accept, decrement on response terminal state (via Drop)
- The guard must be held for the duration of the HTTP response lifecycle. Drop occurs when response reaches terminal state (body fully sent, connection closed, or error). NOT on handler function return — response may outlive handler (e.g., SSE streams)
- Must be cancellation-safe — Drop always runs, even on task cancel
- Count→0: start timeout timer. Timer holds Weak ref to session map (prevents keeping dead sessions alive)
- New request during active timeout: cancel timer, reactivate session
- Timer fires: check Weak ref still valid + access count still 0 before removing

## Cleanup Callback
```rust
type CleanupFn = Box<dyn Fn(SessionId) + Send + Sync>;
```
Invoked when sessions are removed, allowing gateway to clean up per-session resources.

## DELETE Handler
- Transition session to Closing state, return 200 OK (or 204 No Content)
- DELETE is idempotent: DELETE on already Closing/Closed returns success
- NOT 202 (PRD specifies 200/204)

## Max Sessions
Default: 1024 concurrent sessions. Enforced in session creation. Return 503 when limit reached.

## Global Memory Semaphore
256MB global memory budget shared across all sessions (see US-005). Session creation checks semaphore.

## Session ID Generation
Use `uuid::Uuid::new_v4()` — cryptographically random, unguessable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session CRUD: create (with child spawn), get, delete
- [ ] State machine: (none) → Active → Closing → Closed
- [ ] Closing state: reject new requests with 503 + JSON-RPC -32000, drain in-flight up to 5s, then Closed
- [ ] Closed state: session removed from map, subsequent requests return 404
- [ ] DELETE: return 200/204, transition to Closing. Idempotent (DELETE on Closing/Closed returns success)
- [ ] Access counting with RAII guard (Drop on response terminal state, cancellation-safe)
- [ ] Timeout: Weak ref timer, cancel on new request, only fires when access count = 0
- [ ] Session timeout: configurable via --sessionTimeout flag
- [ ] Max sessions (1024 default): return 503 when limit reached
- [ ] Session ID: uuid::Uuid::new_v4(), cryptographically random
- [ ] Global memory semaphore integration: check on session creation
- [ ] Cleanup callback: invoked when session transitions to Closed
- [ ] Session stored inside Asupersync Region — Region cancel kills child
- [ ] Unit tests with LabRuntime (deterministic virtual time): timeout fire, timeout cancel, concurrent inc/dec, clear during timeout, max sessions, state machine transitions, DELETE idempotency, concurrent POST+DELETE race

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings

title: US-007: CORS and Headers
description:
CORS middleware and custom header injection for all server modes.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync middleware/layer pattern (per US-000 findings)
- `regex` crate for regex origin matching
- Applied to all HTTP routes in server modes

## File: src/cors.rs

## CORS Behavior (Three States)
1. `--cors` absent: No CORS headers at all. No OPTIONS handling.
2. `--cors` (present, no values): `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` on all responses. Handle OPTIONS preflight with 204.
3. `--cors origin1 origin2`: Reflect `Origin` header if it's in the allowed list. Add `Vary: Origin` header. Handle OPTIONS preflight with 204.

## Origin Matching Types
Origins can be specified as:
- Exact string match: `https://example.com`
- Regex pattern: `/pattern/` syntax (compiled via regex crate, matched against request Origin)
- Wildcard: `*` (handled by AllowAll state above)

## CorsConfig
```rust
enum CorsConfig {
    Disabled,                              // --cors absent
    AllowAll,                              // --cors with no values
    AllowList(Vec<OriginMatcher>),         // --cors origin1 /regex/ origin2
}

enum OriginMatcher {
    Exact(String),
    Regex(regex::Regex),
}
```

## Credentials Safety
Wildcard origin (`*`) MUST NOT be combined with `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials`. If credentials are needed, reflect the specific origin instead of using wildcard.

## Preflight Response (OPTIONS)
- Status: 204 No Content
- `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`: * or reflected origin
- `Access-Control-Allow-Methods`: GET, POST, DELETE, OPTIONS
- `Access-Control-Allow-Headers`: Content-Type, Accept, Mcp-Session-Id, Last-Event-ID
- `Access-Control-Expose-Headers`: Mcp-Session-Id (when stateful HTTP mode — set via `expose_session_header: bool`)
- `Access-Control-Max-Age`: 86400 (default, configurable)
- `Vary: Origin` (when using AllowList mode)

## Custom Headers (--header)
- Applied to ALL HTTP responses: SSE streams, POST responses, health/readiness, Streamable HTTP, error responses (4xx, 5xx)
- Injected after CORS headers
- Format parsed by CLI (US-002): Vec<(String, String)>
- Last-wins semantics for duplicate keys

## --oauth2Bearer
Adds `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header to outgoing requests. Only when token is actually provided (non-empty). Fixes TS bug D-003.

## Max Header Size
Max 8KB per header. Reject oversized headers with HTTP 431 Request Header Fields Too Large.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CorsConfig enum with three states (Disabled, AllowAll, AllowList)
- [ ] Origin matching: exact strings AND regex patterns (/pattern/ syntax)
- [ ] CORS disabled: no CORS headers, no OPTIONS handling
- [ ] CORS AllowAll: `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` on all responses
- [ ] CORS AllowList: reflect Origin if matched (exact or regex), add `Vary: Origin`
- [ ] Credentials safety: wildcard origin NOT combined with Allow-Credentials

title: US-008: Logging and Observability
description:
Logging infrastructure and observability counters for all modes.

## Tech Stack
- Custom Logger struct wrapping stderr/stdout routing
- AtomicU64 counters for all metrics
- No external logging framework (keep deps minimal)

## Files: src/observe.rs (counters), src/logger.rs (logging)

## Log Routing
- When outputTransport is `stdio`: ALL logs go to stderr (stdout is the MCP transport)
- Otherwise: info to stdout, error to stderr
- Wrap in a Logger struct that routes based on configured output transport

## Log Format
All messages prefixed with `[supergateway]`:
- `[supergateway] [LEVEL] message`
- The `[supergateway]` prefix is REQUIRED on all log messages (PRD line 339)

## Log Levels
- `debug`: verbose protocol messages, full JSON-RPC message bodies, counter snapshots, header values
- `info`: startup, connections, mode transitions, counter changes. NEVER log header values or full payloads at info level (security: may contain auth tokens)
- `none`: suppress all output

## Startup Message
On startup, log at info level (format may differ from TS — intentional divergence):
```
[supergateway] supergateway v<version> | <inputMode> → <outputMode> | port <port>
```

## Counter API
```rust
pub struct Metrics {
    pub active_sessions: AtomicU64,
    pub active_children: AtomicU64,
    pub active_clients: AtomicU64,
    pub total_requests: AtomicU64,
    pub total_spawns: AtomicU64,
    pub spawn_failures: AtomicU64,
    pub session_timeouts: AtomicU64,
    pub forced_kills: AtomicU64,
    pub client_disconnects: AtomicU64,
    pub backpressure_events: AtomicU64,
    pub queue_depth_max: AtomicU64,
    pub decode_errors: AtomicU64,
}
```
Shared as `Arc<Metrics>` across all gateway tasks.

## Counter Change Logging
Gauge counters (active_sessions, active_children, active_clients) logged at **info level** on change (PRD line 271). Use `inc_and_log()` / `dec_and_log()` wrappers.

## Health Endpoint
- `GET <configured-path>`: returns "ok" (plain text) when ready, 503 when not ready
- Readiness conditions: child started (server modes), transport bound, relay loop active
- `GET <configured-path>?detail=true` with `--logLevel debug`: returns JSON metrics snapshot (Content-Type: application/json)
- Without debug or ?detail: plain "ok" text

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Logger struct with stderr/stdout routing based on output transport
- [ ] All messages prefixed with `[supergateway]`
- [ ] Log levels: debug, info, none
- [ ] No header values at info level (security — only at debug)
- [ ] No full JSON-RPC payloads at info level (only method names and IDs)
- [ ] Startup message with version, mode, port
- [ ] Metrics struct with all counters as AtomicU64
- [ ] `inc_and_log()` / `dec_and_log()` wrappers logging at info level on gauge change
- [ ] Arc<Metrics> shared across tasks
- [ ] Health readiness gating: 503 until child started + transport bound + relay active
- [ ] Health detail: JSON metrics snapshot when debug + ?detail=true
- [ ] Test: logger routes to stderr when output=stdio
- [ ] Test: metrics increment/decrement/snapshot
- [ ] Test: health returns 503 before ready, 200 after
- [ ] Test: health detail returns JSON with all counter fields
- [ ] Test: no header values in info-level log output

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
notes:
Counter implementation pattern:
```rust
pub struct Metrics {
    pub active_sessions: AtomicU64,
    pub active_children: AtomicU64,
    pub active_clients: AtomicU64,
    pub total_requests: AtomicU64,
    pub total_spawns: AtomicU64,
    pub spawn_failures: AtomicU64,
    pub session_timeouts: AtomicU64,
    pub forced_kills: AtomicU64,
    pub client_disconnects: AtomicU64,
    pub backpressure_events: AtomicU64,
    pub queue_depth_max: AtomicU64,
    pub decode_errors: AtomicU64,
}

impl Metrics {
    pub fn inc(&self, counter: &AtomicU64) { counter.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed); }
    pub fn dec(&self, gauge: &AtomicU64) { gauge.fetch_sub(1, Ordering::Relaxed); }
    pub fn snapshot(&self) -> MetricsSnapshot { /* read all fields */ }
}
```
Shared as Arc<Metrics> across all gateway tasks. All gateways call these methods.
Health detail endpoint calls snapshot() and serializes to JSON.

Log routing: Use eprintln! for all log output when outputTransport is stdio.
Use println! for info, eprintln! for error otherwise. Wrap in a Logger struct.
